title: atossa sepium var. ochroleuca
intro: Atossa sepium var. ochroleuca, also known as the yellow-leaved or yellow-fruited horse chestnut, is a variety of the Aesculus hippocastanum tree, native to the mountainous regions of the Balkans. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its aescin, a saponin compound, which has anti-inflammatory and anti-swelling properties. The health benefits of Atossa sepium var. ochroleuca include reducing swelling in the legs and feet, relieving varicose veins, and improving circulation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of extracts, capsules, or creams, and are often used in combination with other herbal remedies to support cardiovascular health.
